name: SLB N.V.

profile.description: SLB N.V. (formerly Schlumberger Limited) is a global energy technology company operating through four divisions: Digital & Integration, Reservoir Performance, Well Construction, and Production Systems. It provides field development, hydrocarbon production, carbon management, digital solutions, drilling and completion services, and subsea production systems. The company also offers AI-powered platforms for subsurface insights and production optimization. Founded in 1926, headquartered in Houston, Texas.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): SLB executed a significant strategic transformation, rebranding from Schlumberger in 2022 and repositioning from an oilfield services company to a global energy technology company. The acquisition of ChampionX ($7.8B, closed 2025) and Aker Solutions subsea business expanded into production chemicals and subsea systems, opening substantial new revenue streams [1][2]. AI Deployment (2/2): SLB's Digital division generated $640M in Q1 2026 revenue (up 9% YoY) with Digital Operations growing 87% YoY. Annual recurring revenue surpassed $1B, demonstrating AI and digital products are generating meaningful, quantifiable revenue [1]. CEO Le Peuch stated 'AI is the X factor for our industry' and SLB is deploying AI at scale for subsurface modeling and drilling optimization. Market Share (1/2): SLB is the world's largest oilfield services company with operations in 100+ countries, maintaining leadership positions across most service lines. However, organic revenue declined 7% YoY in Q1 2026 excluding ChampionX, indicating share pressure in the base business [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): SLB has specific next-generation products in its Digital platform with growing ARR, and the ChampionX integration is expected to drive cross-selling opportunities, though specific product launch timelines are limited [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): SLB maintains industry-leading R&D investment for the oilfield services sector, supporting its technology differentiation strategy.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): SLB's purpose — 'driving energy innovation for a balanced planet' — is specific, thoughtful, and distinctive among energy services peers. It explicitly connects the company's work to both energy innovation and environmental balance, reflecting genuine intentionality. The purpose was validated by the strategic transformation from Schlumberger to SLB and the investments in carbon management technology. Leadership references it in strategic decisions. However, it has not been tested through multi-decade consistency in its current form [2][3]. Community Impact (1/2): SLB maintains sustainability programs with Board-level oversight through the VP of Sustainability, and has committed to emissions reduction targets. However, specific charitable programs and community investment dollar amounts are not prominently disclosed beyond standard corporate sustainability reporting [4]. Employee Retention (1/2): SLB employs 109,000 globally with a 'Living our Culture' program designed to embed the new company identity. Some Glassdoor reviews praise innovation and cutting-edge technology exposure while others describe a 'performance-driven' high-pressure environment with demanding targets [3]. Insider Buying (1/2): No significant net insider buying identified in the last 12 months.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics using dividend sleeve. Q1 2026 revenue of $8.72B was up 3% YoY (but down 7% organically excluding ChampionX), with net income of $752M declining 6% YoY [1]. Full-year 2025 revenue of $35.7B declined slightly from $36.3B in 2024. The 5-year revenue CAGR of 8.6% from $23.6B in 2020 reflects the recovery cycle. D/E of 42.5% is well below the 70% target. ROE of 14.1% is near the 15% target. Profit margin of 9.3% is below the 20% target, typical of oilfield services. Forward P/E of 15.9 is below target. Payout ratio of 50.7% is above the 40% target, and consecutive years of dividend growth is only 4 (post-2020 cut), limiting dividend metric scores. The 5-year dividend CAGR of 5.4% is close to the 6% target [1][5].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): SLB's core business — providing physical equipment, engineering services, and proprietary technology for oil and gas extraction — operates in challenging subsurface and subsea environments that AI cannot substitute. Drilling wells, managing reservoir pressure, and deploying subsea production systems require physical infrastructure, specialized crews, and regulatory-compliant operations that are immune to AI displacement [1]. SLB's proprietary subsurface data accumulated over decades creates a defensible data moat. AI enhances SLB's service delivery through digital twins, real-time optimization, and predictive maintenance rather than threatening it. No analysts have flagged AI substitution risk. AI Opportunity (1/1): SLB's Digital division generated $640M in Q1 2026 revenue with $1.02B in ARR (up 15% YoY), representing quantifiable AI-driven revenue through digital operations, subsurface modeling, and AI-powered analytics platforms — the most advanced AI monetization in the oilfield services sector [1].

infinite_game.summary: SLB under Olivier Le Peuch is the most forward-looking player in oilfield services, executing a genuine strategic transformation from Schlumberger to SLB with a repositioned purpose around energy innovation for a balanced planet. The company's willingness to rebrand, invest in carbon management technology, and build a digital platform demonstrates existential flexibility rare in the energy sector. However, the transformation is still early (post-2022) and the company remains overwhelmingly dependent on traditional oil and gas revenue.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): SLB's purpose — 'driving energy innovation for a balanced planet' — is a genuine and distinctive cause that connects energy services to environmental sustainability. Le Peuch references it in strategic decisions, and the company's investments in carbon management, digital platforms, and the corporate rebranding demonstrate it shapes behavior. It differentiates SLB from HAL and Baker Hughes. However, it was articulated in 2022 and has not yet been tested through a full industry cycle or leadership transition [2][3].

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): The rebranding from Schlumberger (a 96-year-old name) to SLB was a courageous symbolic decision signaling strategic transformation. The $7.8B ChampionX acquisition and investments in carbon management technology represent long-term bets. However, SLB has not walked away from profitable traditional business — it has added adjacencies while maintaining the core, reducing the relative courage required [1][2].

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): SLB launched the 'Living our Culture' program to embed the new identity across 109,000 employees, demonstrating intentional culture-building. Some employees praise innovation and technology exposure. However, the performance-driven culture creates high pressure, and the oilfield services industry inherently involves cyclical headcount adjustments that undermine long-term trust. The transformation itself creates uncertainty for employees whose roles may evolve [3].

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): SLB's transformation from Schlumberger — the world's largest oilfield services company — into a self-described global energy technology company is a genuine existential pivot. The company abandoned its 96-year brand identity, invested in carbon management technology, built a $1B+ digital ARR business, acquired ChampionX to expand into production chemicals, and repositioned its purpose around energy innovation rather than oilfield services. This is the kind of proactive reinvention Sinek celebrates [1][2][3].

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): SLB competes with Halliburton and Baker Hughes while using competitive pressure to drive technology differentiation. The company's competitive language focuses on leading energy innovation rather than defeating rivals, and Le Peuch frames the competitive landscape in terms of advancing the industry [2].

investment_thesis: SLB is executing the most ambitious strategic transformation in oilfield services, repositioning from a legacy wireline and drilling company into a global energy technology platform [1][2]. The Digital division's $1.02B ARR (up 15% YoY) validates the technology strategy, while the ChampionX acquisition adds $3.4B+ in annualized production chemicals revenue. At 15.9x forward earnings with a 2.2% dividend yield, SLB offers a balanced return profile. The company's international diversification (100+ countries) and leadership in subsea production systems through OneSubsea provide structural growth drivers as deepwater activity accelerates globally.
thesis_continued: The primary concern is organic revenue decline — excluding ChampionX, Q1 2026 revenue fell 7% YoY, suggesting the base business is under pressure from reduced Middle East activity and customer capital discipline [1]. The PEG ratio of 1.82 indicates the stock is expensive relative to near-term growth expectations. Profit margin of 9.3% is thin for a technology company, and the payout ratio of 50.7% limits capital flexibility. The energy transition represents both an opportunity (carbon management, digital) and a risk (declining long-term oil and gas investment). SLB's transformation is still early — the company remains ~85% dependent on traditional oil and gas revenue, and the new identity has not been stress-tested through a downturn.
key_catalysts: Digital division ARR growing at 15%+ annually, with AI-powered subsurface analytics and digital twins driving higher-margin recurring revenue
ChampionX integration driving cross-selling opportunities and production chemicals revenue contribution of $3.4B+ annually
Deepwater activity acceleration globally benefiting SLB's OneSubsea franchise and Well Construction division
key_risks: Organic revenue declined 7% YoY in Q1 2026 excluding ChampionX, indicating base business pressure from Middle East disruptions and customer spending discipline
PEG ratio of 1.82 and earnings declining 13.8% YoY indicate the stock is priced for growth that has not yet materialized in the base business
Energy transition uncertainty — while SLB has invested in carbon management and digital, ~85% of revenue remains tied to traditional oil and gas activity
sources: SLB Q1 2026 Earnings Release — SEC 8-K Filing, April 24, 2026
SLB Corporate Transformation and Strategy — LinkedIn, Nasdaq, EnergyNow, 2024-2026
SLB Culture Evolution, Glassdoor Reviews, and CEO Le Peuch Leadership — Glassdoor, Oil & Gas IQ, 2024-2026
SLB ESG Ratings and Sustainability Governance — Sustainalytics, slb.com, Yahoo Finance, 2025
